author: lOrm
content: fix: persist_to_disk uses OpenOptions::create_new(true).mode(0o600).open() on Unix — atomic O_EXCL|O_CREAT|O_WRONLY at owner-only perms in a single syscall. Closes both the mid-write 0o644 window and the .tmp symlink-clobber surface. Error paths now remove the half-written tmp. Non-Unix targets refuse to persist (logged error). Four #[cfg(unix)] regression tests added: (1) destination is 0o600 under permissive umask, (2) tmp file never observable at non-0600 mode across a 500-iter race vs a watcher thread, (3) pre-existing .tmp causes fail-closed (attacker file untouched, destination not written), (4) round-trip control proving token bytes do reach disk. cargo test --lib oauth::: 20 pass / 0 fail. No allow attrs, no clippy.toml, no #[ignore].
